# Fully pinned + hash-pinned so `pip install --require-hashes` (see Dockerfile)
# satisfies the Scorecard Pinned-Dependencies check (alert: pipCommand not
# pinned by hash). Hashes cover all wheels + sdist per version, so the lock is
# platform-portable.
#
# Regenerate after editing requirements.in (resolved in the runtime image,
# linux/amd64, for a faithful transitive closure):
#   docker run --rm --platform linux/amd64 -v "$PWD":/w -w /w python:3.11-slim \
#     bash -c 'pip install -q uv==0.5.31 && uv pip compile --generate-hashes \
#       --no-header --no-emit-index-url requirements.in -o requirements.txt'
#   (then re-add this header block)
